did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper utilized artificial intelligence for the detection and diagnosis of basal cell carcinomas (BCC) in Gorlin syndrome patients. Specifically, it employed AI-assisted videodermoscopy using the DEXI application to provide malignancy scores and AI-derived confirmation using a ConvNeXt model on LC-OCT images to non-invasively confirm BCC diagnosis and establish surgical margins.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper describes original experimental and observational studies. Specifically, it reports on a clinical retrospective study of 3 Gorlin syndrome patients using a multimodal imaging workflow (VECTRA 3D, DEXI, LC-OCT) and a prospective single-center observational study of 263 cutaneous lesions. It presents new results, including diagnostic accuracy metrics (100% sensitivity, 90% specificity) and detailed imaging findings, rather than summarizing prior literature.

title: Multimodal skin imaging with VECTRA®3D total body photography associated with videodermoscopy and line-field confocal optical coherence tomography coupled with artificial intelligence for difficult to diagnose basal cell carcinomas in Gorlin patients
brief_summary: This study evaluated a multimodal imaging workflow combining VECTRA® 3D total body photography, AI-assisted videodermoscopy (DEXI), and line-field confocal optical coherence tomography (LC-OCT) for diagnosing basal cell carcinomas (BCC) in Gorlin syndrome patients. The research included a retrospective analysis of three Gorlin syndrome patients and a prospective observational study of 263 cutaneous lesions. AI tools provided malignancy scores and ConvNeXt-based confirmation, while LC-OCT offered in vivo cellular resolution to establish surgical margins. The multimodal approach achieved 100% sensitivity and 90% specificity for BCC detection, significantly reducing unnecessary biopsies. One-step surgical management was facilitated in 93% of confirmed BCC cases, demonstrating the workflow's efficacy in improving diagnosis and treatment planning for difficult-to-diagnose lesions.

all_task_types.target: Malignancy status (benign vs malignant) and specific diagnostic categories (e.g., Basal Cell Carcinoma, Actinic Keratosis).
all_task_types.details: The study utilized AI-assisted videodermoscopy (DEXI application) to provide 'malignancy scores' for lesion classification. A ConvNeXt model was used on LC-OCT images for 'AI-derived confirmation' of Basal Cell Carcinoma (BCC) diagnosis. The workflow achieved 100% sensitivity and 90% specificity for BCC detection, indicating a binary/multiclass diagnostic classification task.

datasets_used.name: Gorlin Syndrome (GS) Retrospective Cohort
datasets_used.description: A clinical retrospective study cohort of 3 Gorlin syndrome patients diagnosed and followed up in the dermatology department since 2015, evaluated using a multimodal imaging workflow (VECTRA 3D, DEXI, LC-OCT) starting in 2022.

use.testing: Used for evaluation of the multimodal imaging workflow's diagnostic accuracy and surgical management facilitation.
use.other: Retrospective analysis to evaluate the impact of monitoring with the innovative workflow.

datasets_used.name: Prospective Single-Center Observational Study of Cutaneous Lesions
datasets_used.description: A prospective observational study involving 263 cutaneous lesions suspicious for neoplasia, examined using dermoscopy, dynamic optical coherence tomography (D-OCT), and line-field confocal optical coherence tomography (LC-OCT). This cohort included 29 difficult-to-diagnose lesions analyzed in the specific multimodal workflow approach since 2022.

use.testing: Used to characterize entity-specific vascular profiles and evaluate the diagnostic performance of multimodal imaging (dermoscopy, D-OCT, LC-OCT).

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. It reported descriptive statistics and diagnostic accuracy metrics (100% sensitivity, 90% specificity) without statistical inference.

Gorlin Syndrome Retrospective Cohort.metric: Descriptive statistics of patient demographics and lesion characteristics

VECTRA 3D + DEXI + LC-OCT.lesion_characteristics: Multiple pigmented and non-pigmented lesions <5 mm without specific dermoscopy criteria

Gorlin Syndrome Retrospective Cohort.notes: The study included 3 Gorlin syndrome patients diagnosed and followed up since 2015. Since 2022, a multimodal screening approach using VECTRA 3D, AI-assisted videodermoscopy (DEXI), and LC-OCT was applied. The workflow enabled non-invasive confirmation of BCC diagnosis and establishment of surgical safety margins before removal surgery.

Prospective Single-Center Observational.metric: Diagnostic accuracy, histological confirmation counts, and surgical management facilitation

DEXI_application.function: Provided malignancy scores via AI-assisted videodermoscopy integrated within VECTRA 3D
DEXI_application.result_description: High AI score observed in difficult-to-diagnose lesions with non-specific dermoscopic criteria

ConvNeXt.function: AI-derived confirmation of BCC diagnosis on LC-OCT images
ConvNeXt.result_description: Enabled non-invasive confirmation of BCC diagnosis

Prospective Single-Center Observational.notes: In the prospective study of 263 lesions, 29 difficult-to-diagnose lesions were analyzed. All lesions suspected to be BCC were surgically excised. The multimodal approach achieved 100% sensitivity and 90% specificity for BCC detection, reducing unnecessary biopsies. One-step surgical management was facilitated in 93% of confirmed BCC cases. Entity-specific vascular profiles were identified: BCCs showed large-caliber vessels, high flow intensity, and frequent intravascular rolling; SCC/Bowen's showed glomerular/punctate patterns; Melanoma/Melanoma metastasis showed polymorphic vascular architectures; Benign lesions showed low inflammatory activity.

architecture_details.application: AI-derived confirmation of Basal Cell Carcinoma (BCC) diagnosis on LC-OCT images
architecture_details.integration: Integrated within the VECTRA® 3D workflow for LC-OCT analysis

classification_or_output_head.details: Used to provide AI-derived confirmation of BCC diagnosis non-invasively

conclusions: The innovative multimodal non-invasive imaging workflow combining VECTRA 3D videodermoscopy with AI and LC-OCT enhances early basal cell carcinoma (BCC) diagnosis in complex Gorlin syndrome patients, improving diagnosis, follow-up, and surgical management.
The multimodal approach achieved 100% sensitivity and 90% specificity for BCC detection, thereby reducing unnecessary biopsies.
One-step surgical management was facilitated in 93% of confirmed BCC cases using this workflow.
The workflow contributed to reducing unnecessary surgery and supported diagnostic training for less experienced clinicians.

The combination of dermoscopy, dynamic optical coherence tomography (D-OCT), and line-field confocal optical coherence tomography (LC-OCT) enables a differentiated, depth- and function-sensitive characterization of tumor-associated microvasculature.
Multimodal vascular imaging substantially broadens noninvasive diagnostic insight into cutaneous neoplasms.
Quantitative perfusion parameters and functional LC-OCT features, including intravascular rolling, represent promising imaging biomarkers for future diagnostic refinement and therapy monitoring.
important_remarks: The study is a conference abstract (A-299) rather than a full peer-reviewed paper, limiting the depth of methodological detail and statistical reporting.
The retrospective component of the study has a very small sample size (n=3 Gorlin syndrome patients), which limits the generalizability of the workflow's impact on patient monitoring.
The prospective observational study (n=263 lesions) is single-center, introducing potential selection bias and limiting external validation.

The AI model (ConvNeXt) used for LC-OCT analysis lacks details on training data, pre-training, or validation splits, raising concerns about potential data leakage or overfitting if the same institution's data was used for both development and testing.

No statistical significance tests (p-values) or confidence intervals were reported for the diagnostic accuracy metrics (100% sensitivity, 90% specificity), making it impossible to assess the precision of these estimates.
The '100% sensitivity' result is based on a small number of confirmed BCCs (n=26) in the prospective cohort, which may not reflect performance in broader populations.
The workflow relies on specialized, expensive equipment (VECTRA 3D, D-OCT, LC-OCT), which may limit its clinical deployment to specialized centers.
The study focuses on 'difficult-to-diagnose' lesions and Gorlin syndrome patients, so the results may not be directly applicable to routine primary care settings with typical skin lesions.
